summary: P0 Bug Fix Verified: Tenant query fix working correctly. Tenant B users now see all products (91) from the global automate-accounts catalog. All backend API tests passed (16/16). Store and Articles UI confirmed working for authenticated Tenant B users.
passed_tests: Tenant B (adminb@tenantb.local) GET /api/products returns 91 products (global catalog)

Tenant B GET /api/categories returns 11 categories (Zoho Express Setup, Audit & Optimize, Build & Automate, Accounting on Zoho, Managed Services, Migrate to Zoho, CRM, SaaS Products, Setup & Onboarding, Consulting Services, Test)
Tenant B products include automate-accounts tenant_id entries
Platform admin GET /api/products returns 75 products (no duplication)
Platform admin products have no duplicate product IDs
Platform admin GET /api/products with X-View-As-Tenant (Tenant B) returns 91 products
Tenant B GET /api/articles/public returns 13 articles from both tenant_b AND automate-accounts
Platform admin GET /api/articles/public with X-View-As-Tenant (Tenant B) works correctly
Tenant B products have no duplicate product IDs
Tenant B categories have no duplicates
Unauthenticated GET /api/categories returns global catalog categories
Store page UI (Tenant B): Shows all 11 categories with correct product counts
Store page UI (Tenant B): Products from automate-accounts catalog visible (Zoho Express Setup, CRM, etc.)
Articles page UI (Tenant B): Shows 13 articles total (All: 13, Blog: 2, Scope-Final Won: 2, Setup Guides: 6)
Articles page UI (Tenant B): Shows articles from both Tenant B ('TB Article (Audit Test)') and automate-accounts ('AA Article')
action_items: OPTIONAL: store.py get_product (line 129) still uses single tenant_id - if Tenant B user tries to access product detail page for automate-accounts product, it may 404. Consider applying same $in fix.
OPTIONAL: store.py orders/preview (line 249) uses single tenant_id for product lookup - verify checkout flow works for Tenant B purchasing global catalog products.
critical_code_review_comments: store.py: get_product (line 129) still uses single tenant_id filter - if a Tenant B user navigates to a specific product detail page, the product may not be found since tenant_id='automate-accounts' won't match Tenant B's tenant_id.
store.py: orders/preview (line 249) uses single tenant_id filter for product lookup - Tenant B checkout for global catalog products may fail with 404.
context_for_next_testing_agent: P0 bug fix verified. The fix in store.py (lines 63, 86-89) and articles.py (line 127) correctly uses $in: [tid, DEFAULT_TENANT_ID] to include both user's tenant_id AND automate-accounts in queries. Exact counts: Tenant B products=91, categories=11, articles=13. Platform admin products=75 (no duplication). Two optional edge cases noted: get_product and orders/preview still use single tenant_id filter - these may need $in fix for product detail/checkout flows for Tenant B.
